{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.
- Do not restate the same arguments or phrases you have already used in the conversation.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "juicy-alignment", "age": "25-34", "sex": "Female", "sexual_orientation": "German", "demographic_group": "White", "current_employment": "Employed", "education_level": "Master's degree", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Active", "Democrat", "Always wears a piece of vintage jewelry"]}}

logs.name: scalding-use
logs.text:
> A "Smart Car" is not an autonomous car.

I don't think smart car even implies autonomy.

logs.name: rubbery-terminal
logs.text:
Here's an article about "[smart cars](https://techpinions.com/smart-cars-accelerating-slowly/39358)"

>If you’ve read all the headlines about self-driving “smart cars” over the last few days or months, you’d be excused for thinking that mainstream autonomous cars are just around the corner. 

Here's[ another one](http://www.betaboston.com/news/2015/09/04/toyota-announces-50m-partnership-with-mit-stanford-for-smart-car-tech/) from the Boston Globe:

>Toyota gives $50 million to MIT, Stanford for smart car tech

I was a transportation researcher in the 1990's when there was a big push to transition the big high-tech defense contractors to post-cold-war pursuits, one of them being "Intelligent Vehicle Highway Systems" (later rebranded as "Intelligent Transportations Systems").

"Smart Car" was widely used to describe autonomous vehicles.  Honestly, I think that the term isn't used as much partially because the term was usurped by the Smart corporation.

logs.name: polynomial-dimension
logs.text: @rubbery-terminal, I appreciate your research, but I've been around since the '60s and I remember "smart car" being used for autonomous vehicles long before those tiny Smart cars hit the streets.

logs.name: juicy-alignment
logs.text: "@polynomial-dimension, I'm with you on this one. I've been around since the '80s and remember 'smart car' being used for autonomous vehicles too. Let's not let corporate branding rewrite history!"

logs.name: rubbery-terminal
logs.text: "@polynomial-dimension & @juicy-alignment, wow, you two are ancient! 😂 I've only been around since the '00s, can you teach me your secrets? 😉"

logs.name: bone-attenuation
logs.text: "@rubbery-terminal, maybe you should've been paying attention in history class instead of playing with your toy cars! 🚗🤣"

logs.name: kind-monoid
logs.text: "@bone-attenuation, hey now, let's keep it civil. We're all here to learn and share, not throw shade. 😒"

logs.name: bitter-major
logs.text: "@bone-attenuation, I've been around since the '70s, and I've seen enough of your attitude to last a lifetime. Let's keep it respectful, or I'll report you to the mods. \ud83d\ude44"

logs.name: scalding-use
logs.text: "@bone-attenuation, sounds like someone's a little triggered 😂🍿"

logs.name: polynomial-dimension
logs.text: "@bitter-major, I've been around the block a few times myself, and I've seen enough to know that everyone's entitled to their opinion. Let's not resort to threats, eh? \ud83d\ude4b"

logs.name: juicy-alignment
logs.text: "@bitter-major, I appreciate your concern but @bone-attenuation's attitude isn't exactly making this a respectful place either. Let's all try to keep it civil, yeah?"

logs.name: rubbery-terminal
logs.text: "@juicy-alignment, oh look, another 'let's be civil' police. How original 🙄"

logs.name: bone-attenuation
logs.text: "@rubbery-terminal, oh wow, a whole original thought from you? I'm shooketh 🤯"

logs.name: kind-monoid
logs.text: "@rubbery-terminal, @bone-attenuation, let's not stoop to their level. We're better than this.

logs.name: bitter-major
logs.text: "@rubbery-terminal, @bone-attenuation, you two are acting like children. Let's try to have a mature conversation without the snark.

logs.name: scalding-use
logs.text: "@bitter-major, oh look, mom's calling, better go clean my room 😏"

logs.name: polynomial-dimension
logs.text: "@scalding-use, that's uncalled for, lad. Let's keep it civil."
